title: Interactive Titration Calculator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Slider-driven titration tool that compares strong and weak acid titrations, calculates equivalence volumes, and renders the titration curve instantly.

learningObjectives: Apply M_a V_a = M_b V_b to predict the required titrant volume at equivalence.
Analyze how acid strength changes the titration curve shape and equivalence pH.

keyQuestions: How does the equivalence volume change when Ca or Cb is adjusted?
Why is the equivalence pH above 7 for weak acids titrated with strong base?
commonMisconceptions: Equivalence pH is always 7 regardless of acid strength.
Volume at equivalence depends on both Ca and Cb in a non-linear way.
assessmentOpportunities: Students submit curve screenshots alongside calculated volumes.
Learners explain how the simulation reflects Henderson–Hasselbalch in the buffer region.
